title: Japan falls to Brazil and says goodbye to the World Cup in the group stage
summary: The Japanese national team lost after a comeback by the 'Canarinha' in its last match, being eliminated in the first round despite massive fan support.

title: Japanese Government approves reform of the Imperial Household Law to ensure succession
summary: The bill allows women of the imperial family to maintain their status after marriage and the adoption of males from former collateral branches to secure succession to the throne.

title: Yen plunges to its lowest level in 39 and a half years, surpassing 162 units per dollar
summary: The Japanese currency continues to weaken against the greenback amid expectations that the Bank of Japan will maintain its loose monetary policy, prompting statements of alert from the Government.

title: Bear attacks increase in Japan with multiple injured and a bullet train hit
summary: Several people were injured in attacks in Kyoto and Tochigi prefecture, while a train on the Akita Shinkansen line collided with a bear without causing casualties among passengers.

title: Air Self-Defense Force will keep its abbreviation 'Kūji' despite its name change
summary: After the creation of the Aerospace Self-Defense Force, the Government decided to maintain the abbreviation 'Kūji' (空自) to avoid confusion.

title: Japan Airlines must return 300 million yen for fraud in drone subsidies
summary: The Japanese airline improperly received public funds for the research of 'flying cars', which has forced the company to reimburse the money to the State.

title: Daimaru department store in Shimonoseki will close its doors in 2027
summary: The establishment, a symbol in Yamaguchi prefecture, closes after four consecutive years of declining sales, leaving the region without this type of business.

title: Keiko Fujimori wins Peru's presidential election on her fourth attempt
summary: The daughter of former President Alberto Fujimori prevailed in the elections, continuing her father's legacy and promising a government of national unity after a close vote.

title: An earthquake in Venezuela leaves more than 1,700 dead and serious material damage
summary: The earthquake, one of the most devastating in the country's recent history, caused the destruction of tens of thousands of buildings, while Japan announced the sending of humanitarian aid.

title: Controversy in Japan's Parliament over the approval of the law criminalizing flag desecration
summary: The House of Representatives approved the bill in an atypical session without the presence of the co-sponsoring parties, generating an intense debate about freedom of expression.

title: Shohei Ohtani hits his eighteenth home run and closes in on 300 in the Major Leagues
summary: The Japanese star of the Dodgers broke a six-game homerless streak by hitting a three-run home run, now just two away from reaching 300 for his career in MLB.

title: Six people die in a shooting at a youth shelter in northern Germany
summary: The armed attack, which occurred at a social welfare center, may have been motivated by a custody dispute, according to German authorities.

title: Morocco eliminates Netherlands in penalty shootout and advances to the round of 16
summary: The Moroccan team came back in stoppage time to force extra time and ultimately won from the penalty spot in a thrilling World Cup match that left them out of the title race.

title: Naomi Osaka debuts with a convincing victory at Wimbledon
summary: The Japanese tennis player comfortably got through the first round of the London tournament, showing her best level on grass and setting her path in the Grand Slam.

title: Hong Kong launches a special program for the 'rehabilitation' of young people detained in protests
summary: The Government of the Special Administrative Region launched an initiative to support the reintegration of young people arrested during the pro-democracy demonstrations in recent years.

title: Civil war in Myanmar worsens due to the impact of conflicts in the Middle East
summary: The civilian population in Burma is suffering a deterioration of their living conditions due to the combination of internal conflict and international crises, according to residents' reports.

title: US Supreme Court prevents Trump from dismissing a Federal Reserve adviser
summary: The country's highest court ruled against the US president's attempt to immediately fire Fed Governor Christopher Cook, setting a precedent on the independence of the central bank.

title: Aflac suffers massive data breach affecting 4.38 million policyholders
summary: The Japanese-American insurer confirmed that it suffered multiple cyberattacks that exposed personal information of its clients, although no fraudulent use of it has been detected so far.

title: South Korean Prime Minister, ally of the president, will run to lead the ruling party
summary: Kim, a senior government official and close collaborator of President Yoon Suk-yeol, will compete in the People Power Party primaries against the current representative, Jeong.

title: Hundreds of dead dolphins appear in the Black Sea after the Russian invasion of Ukraine
summary: Since the start of the war, a mass die-off of dolphins in the Black Sea has been recorded, attributed to the increase in military naval traffic and noise pollution.
